rca_result.root_cause_signal: CPU saturation at WAF middleware layer
causal_chain: First adversarial request triggers catastrophic backtracking in EVIL regex
Regex engine enters exponential-time loop on '(?:(?:"|(d|.*)+(.*=.*))' vs 'xxxxx=xxxxxx'
Python re module consumes 100% CPU — async uvicorn event loop blocked
All subsequent requests queue behind blocked coroutine

pipeline_gaps_detected.description: Pipeline did not distinguish WAF middleware CPU spike from application-level CPU spike. RCA output said 'api service degraded' but could not identify the specific code path (WAF middleware vs business logic) as root.
pipeline_gaps_detected.impact: Slower mitigation — operator had to manually check middleware logs to find the regex rule. Pipeline pointed to the right service but not the right layer.
pipeline_gaps_detected.mitigation: Add structured WAF middleware metric: 'waf_middleware_duration_seconds' histogram. If WAF processing time > 1s, fire 'WAFRegexSlowPath' alert independently from generic HighLatency.

pipeline_gaps_detected.description: Pipeline alert fired AFTER requests were already blocked. MTTD was 4 seconds from inject, but the first request was already degraded at t+0 (inject time). There was no pre-deploy validation that could catch the malicious regex BEFORE global rollout.
pipeline_gaps_detected.impact: In the real Cloudflare incident, 82% traffic was dropped for 27 minutes before rollback. A pre-deploy regex complexity check (ReDoS detector) would prevent the deploy entirely.
pipeline_gaps_detected.mitigation: Add a 'canary gate' check in the deployment pipeline: run candidate WAF rules through a ReDoS complexity analyzer (e.g., 'safe-regex' or backtracking complexity estimator) before any production deploy. If complexity > O(n^2), block deploy.
